assumptions: The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised monetary receivable in the consolidated financial statements.
The issue is assessed in the consolidated financial statements rather than only in separate financial statements.
The hedge being considered is a hedge of foreign currency risk under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 specifically addresses intragroup monetary assets and liabilities in consolidation and requires exchange differences to be recognised in profit or loss or, in specified cases, in OCI.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets and liabilities as eligible hedged items, making fair value hedge accounting a distinct hedge accounting model potentially relevant to a recognised foreign-currency receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies forecast transactions and aggregated exposures as possible hedged items, so cash flow hedge accounting is a separate IFRS hedge model relevant to foreign currency exposures including certain intragroup transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 establish net investment hedging as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk relating to foreign operations, with OCI treatment and recycling on disposal.
